items.title: Is AI euphoria an overlooked risk for markets beyond geopolitical shocks
items.summary: As AI stocks surge, experts warn of the risks associated with excessive optimism. Transitioning to a value-driven investment strategy focused on fundamentals rather than narratives is crucial. Discover the balance needed to navigate the exhilarating yet perilous landscape of AI investments.

items.title: India Inc gives GST thumbs up; seeks faster refunds, smoother audits: Deloitte Survey
items.summary: The survey calls for the next phase of reform to move beyond digitisation to an AI-driven compliance and data-led dispute reduction

items.title: Personal guarantors find fewer escape routes under corporate insolvency
items.summary: Recent rulings indicate that personal guarantors of corporate loans face increased liability risks when companies default. Legal changes stress that these guarantees remain enforceable despite corporate insolvency, emphasizing the importance of personal guarantees for creditor recovery efforts.

items.title: India, US begin high-level trade talks as two nations rework deal amid tariff changes
items.summary: The talks are focused on reworking a framework agreement announced in February that was built around tariff commitments later rendered uncertain by a US Supreme Court ruling striking down sweeping tariffs announced by Trump

items.title: Tax reforms to play vital role in attracting global capital to India: Experts
items.summary: The experts observe that India's tax administration has made significant strides towards creating a more transparent, technology-driven, and taxpayer-centric ecosystem

items.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
items.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.

items.title: SREI Finance withdraws plea against Nirmal Lifestyle Mulund’s revival plan at appellate tribunal
items.summary: A key creditor has withdrawn its appeal against Nirmal Lifestyle's resolution plan after the buyer, Mantra Properties, clarified a disputed mortgaged property isn't included. The National Company Law Tribunal had approved Mantra's acquisition of the debt-laden firm, which owes Rs 1,491 crore, with the plan proposing Rs 215 crore.

items.title: Adani Mundra Airport launches first scheduled flight services
items.summary: Adani Mundra Airport has launched its first scheduled flight service with Star Air, connecting Mundra to Mumbai and Goa. This expansion includes eight new routes, significantly boosting connectivity for businesses, trade, and tourism in the Kutch region. The airport's new operations are a key step in developing Kutch into a comprehensive logistics and business hub, adding another vital transport option for commercial activities.

items.title: Dollar hits one-year high on Fed hike bets; yen nears 40-year low
items.summary: The US dollar surged to a year-high on Tuesday, driven by expectations of a more aggressive Federal Reserve and ongoing Middle East uncertainty. Traders are anticipating rate hikes, with major banks revising their forecasts. Meanwhile, the Japanese yen teetered near a four-decade low, prompting speculation about potential intervention by Japanese authorities amidst growing concerns over sharp currency swings.

items.title: Why Vedanta shares are falling today: Here's what is behind the 8% drop in the stock
items.summary: The stock emerged among the top losers on the NSE after a large stake sale through block deals hit the market.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live Updates: Sensex drops 650 pts, Nifty below 23,900 as IT, metal stocks weigh
items.summary: Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates: Domestic benchmark indices extended losses in afternoon trade on Tuesday after flat opening, with IT and metal stocks dragging the market despite easing geopolitical tensions in West Asia. Investors turned cautious after the recent rally, while attention shifted to the progress of the monsoon as a key near-term trigger for market direction.

items.title: Gold loans surge 84% in FY26, emerge as mainstream credit driver: Experian
items.summary: Repeat borrowers dominate as ticket sizes double, growth spreads beyond southern markets, and asset quality improves amid rising gold prices and expanding credit access

items.title: India’s electric freight market enters next growth phase as Q1 volumes surge
items.summary: The shift is being driven by economics as much as sustainability. Fleet operators are increasingly evaluating electric vehicles on total cost of ownership, uptime and route suitability rather than environmental goals alone

items.title: Is Kirloskar Oil Engines stock running ahead of fundamentals after 25% jump this week?
items.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines shares hit a 52-week high after winning a 192MW data-centre genset order from HyperNext, breaking into a segment dominated by Cummins. Strong FY26 numbers, capacity expansion and a narrowing valuation gap versus Cummins are fuelling the re-rating.

items.title: Global Market | European shares slip on Fed hike bets, tech drag
items.summary: European markets opened lower Tuesday, impacted by anticipated US Federal Reserve rate hikes and concerns over escalating corporate AI investments.  The STOXX 600 index saw a notable dip, with basic resources and tech sectors leading the decline.  Asian equities also experienced significant drops. Investors are bracing for further interest rate increases globally as inflation persists, particularly from rising energy costs.

items.title: GST wins India Inc's vote, but businesses seek faster refunds and smarter reforms: Deloitte
items.summary: India's GST regime has achieved widespread acceptance, with digitisation and rate adjustments hailed as major successes. Businesses now prioritize clearer interpretations, uniform audits, and faster refunds to ease working capital. The corporate sector is eager for AI-powered compliance tools and a unified taxpayer dashboard, signaling a push for more advanced, business-friendly reforms.

items.title: Who is Kunal Shah? CRED founder set to lead WhatsApp globally
items.summary: Kunal Shah, the entrepreneur behind fintech platform CRED, is set to become the next global leader of WhatsApp, marking one of the most significant leadership transitions in the technology industry. However, Shah clarified that he would continue to be associated with CRED as a shareholder despite stepping away from its operating role.

items.title: Global funds snap Indian stock selling streak as oil shock ebbs
items.summary: Global funds purchased almost $900 million of Indian shares last week following eight consecutive weeks of selling, according to Bloomberg estimates

items.title: US-India Economic Partnership: US Trade representative Jamieson Greer in Delhi for bilateral trade agreement
items.summary: The US Trade Representative was accompanied by the US ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, and a US trade delegation.

items.title: Stock Market Crash: Rs 4.4 lakh crore mcap lost in 1 hour! Why did Sensex, Nifty fall today?
items.summary: Why Stock Market is Falling Today: Indian benchmark indices fell on Tuesday after weakness in global markets, a sharp selloff in technology stocks and losses across Asian markets weighed on investor sentiment. The BSE Sensex declined 506.42 points or 0.66 per cent to 76,587.65. The NSE Nifty fell 188.95 points or 0.78 per cent to 23,913.95 at 1:16 pm.

items.title: Slowing rural incomes could weigh on consumer demand: Dhananjay Sinha
items.summary: Headline rural wage growth in India is misleading, with underlying increases significantly lower than reported.  Rising inflation, reverse migration from cities, and an uncertain monsoon threaten to dampen rural demand.  Consumer companies may face squeezed purchasing power as prices climb and real wages stagnate, potentially impacting their profitability and volume growth in upcoming quarters.

items.title: Defence valuations 'obnoxious'; private banks look strong: Sandip Sabharwal's full market playbook
items.summary: Investor Sandip Sabharwal cautions that while the defence rally is real, many stock prices have outpaced reality. He favors Bharat Electronics but finds many small-cap defence stocks "obnoxious." Pharma offers decent but unexciting prospects. Sabharwal is constructive on private banks, anticipating accelerated growth. IT valuations are attractive but lack clear triggers. In manufacturing, Bharat Forge stands out, though some power equipment firms are overvalued.

items.title: Why is market falling today? Sensex tanks 700 pts, Nifty below 23,900; 5 key triggers behind D-St selloff
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Tuesday, with the Sensex and Nifty seeing substantial losses and wiping out nearly Rs 4.57 lakh crore in investor wealth. This decline was largely influenced by weak global cues, particularly a sharp crash in South Korea's Kospi index and a selloff in technology stocks. Major Indian companies like Infosys and TCS also faced considerable drops, contributing to the market's downward trend.

items.title: Rate cuts transmission moderated in May 2026: RBI
items.summary: Between May 2022 and Jan 2025, RBI increased repo rate by 250 bps. Between February 2025 and April 2026, the repo rate decreased 85 basis points

items.title: Akasa Air eyes 30% capacity growth in FY27
items.summary: Akasa Air is set for a significant 30% capacity expansion this financial year, with ambitious long-term plans remaining steadfast despite geopolitical uncertainties. The airline, operating 39 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft, currently dedicates about a quarter of its capacity to international routes.  With a focus on strong capitalization, Akasa Air is well-positioned to navigate rising operational costs, aiming for 30-40% growth over the next 4-5 years.

items.title: Jio Platforms IPO: RIL to not levy royalty for 'Jio' brand
items.summary: RIL has decided to depart from a common practice among Indian conglomerates, in which promoter entities charge a fee calculated based on the operating company's financials.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank has 'high ambitions' to deploy capital for growth, CEO says
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank is actively pursuing acquisitions, including loan portfolios and alternative assets, to deploy surplus capital and fuel growth. CEO Ashok Vaswani aims for the bank to become India's third-largest private lender by profit. The bank is also investing heavily in technology and AI to enhance efficiency, expecting workforce growth to lag behind balance-sheet expansion.

items.title: IT selloff drags Nifty below 24,000; pharma stocks buck the trend
items.summary: Pharma bucked the broader trend and remained the standout performer of the session. Cipla led gains on the Nifty 50, rising 2.56 per cent to ₹1,452 with over 37.20 lakh shares traded worth ₹53,831.39 lakh

items.title: Meesho rallies as Citi starts coverage with 'buy' rating; what Anil Singhvi says
items.summary: Meesho shares took centre stage in the market after Citi initiated coverage on the e-tailer with a 'buy' rating and a target price of Rs 210 per share.

items.title: Real estate platform Square Yards achieves unicorn status after securing 900 cr in funding
items.summary: Real estate platform Square Yards has secured Rs 900 crore in a funding round, achieving unicorn status with a valuation exceeding $1 billion. This capital infusion, anchored by EAAA Alternatives and Muzinich & Co., will fuel market expansion and technological advancements as the company eyes a Rs 2,000 crore IPO. Square Yards reported robust revenue growth and a significant jump in profitability for FY26.

items.title: Sensex slides over 590 pts, Nifty breaches 24,000 as IT and metal stocks weigh on markets, Infosys AGM in focus
items.summary: Investors turned cautious after the recent rally, while attention shifted to the progress of the monsoon as a key near-term trigger for market direction

items.title: JSW Infrastructure launches Rs 7,503 crore QIP; shares gain over 2% as expansion plans gather pace
items.summary: Infrastructure major launches one of the sector's largest recent QIPs, with proceeds earmarked for expansion, debt repayment and strategic investments.

items.title: Piyush Goyal, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer begin talks on trade pact
items.summary: Discussions aim to finalize the framework of the Bilateral Trade Agreement, addressing recent changes in U.S. tariffs and exploring mutual benefits in key sectors

items.title: Japan's Nikkei slides to one-week low as profit-taking hits tech stocks
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei index tumbled to a one-week low after a streak of all-time high gains, as investors decided to cash in on profits. The pullback was broad-based, primarily driven by declining AI and semiconductor stocks that had previously led the surge. Analysts warn the market was overheating, hinting at an inevitable correction, although some defensive stocks managed to perform well during this downturn.

items.title: Chinese tech, investments welcome, but trade opening needs caution: CII President
items.summary: Chinese investments and technology are welcome in India, but a cautious approach is needed for trade, according to CII President R Mukundan. He emphasized reducing import dependence on China and boosting domestic manufacturing. Mukundan also highlighted potential collaborations in battery storage and semiconductor chemicals. Regarding the US, he stressed the need for India to secure comparable tariff rates in the ongoing trade pact negotiations.

items.title: FCRA Rules tightened: NGOs must declare social media accounts, stick to specified activities; political content barred
items.summary: All NGOs and associations registered under FCRA, 2010 are required to disclose specific activities and geographical scope of their programmes

items.title: Piyush Goyal, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer begin talks on trade pact
items.summary: India and the US are in high-level talks to finalize the first phase of their bilateral trade agreement. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to iron out details, aiming to give a final touch to the framework deal. Both nations are working to close open ends, with President Trump indicating they are "very close" to an agreement, despite recent shifts in US tariff policies.

items.title: IDFC First Bank share price falls over 2% amid weak sentiment in Indian stock market
items.summary: IDFC First Bank share price has risen 14% in one month and has rallied over 30% in three months. The banking stock has fallen 7% in six months, while it has gained 11% in one year. IDFC First Bank shares have surged 36% in five years.

items.title: Explained: Meta’s $900 million investment in Cred and hiring of Kunal Shah as WhatsApp chief
items.summary: The hiring accomplishes the dual aim of investing into a business focused on the few million Indians with a credit card, and getting a leader for WhatsApp at a time it is pushing to leverage the multi-billion user base Meta has been able to amass.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO sees a slow response on Day 1 so far. Should you subscribe?
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism’s IPO opened to a muted response, with subscription at 7% on Day 1 and weak institutional participation. While the company benefits from leadership in India’s cruise market and expansion plans, subdued grey market signals and execution risks have kept investor enthusiasm measured.

items.title: NSE IPO: What the listing means for BSE and its business | Explained
items.summary: In simple terms, every trade in NSE shares after listing will generate business for BSE. The IPO could also draw more investors to BSE's cash market segment, where its market share has remained in the 6-8 per cent range for several years.

items.title: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026): his mixed legacy is traceable to how he viewed the free market’s role
items.summary: Do free markets correct themselves? Both the successes and failures of the former Fed chair revolved around this crucial question. Greenspan’s tenure at the Federal Reserve saw America’s Great Moderation and led up to its Great Recession.

items.title: CRM programme helps Punjab, Haryana villages to move away from stubble burning
items.summary: Awareness campaigns and access to shared farm machinery under a crop residue management helps in a big way

items.title: Chalo Delhi NCR! Adani or Sobha, India's biggest developers are heading to the national capital region
items.summary: India's national developers are significantly boosting their presence in Delhi-NCR's housing market, capturing over 13% of new supply by 2025, a jump from just 3% in 2022. Homebuyers increasingly favor established brands and large listed builders, driving this shift. This trend signals a more institutionalized and brand-driven NCR housing sector, with Gurugram emerging as the top destination for these expanding players.

items.title: India emerges as a hybrid video entertainment economy, driven by shifting audience preferences
items.summary: India's entertainment landscape is transforming into a hybrid model, with audiences now dictating content, business strategies, and viewing habits. Companies are blending subscriptions, advertising, commerce, and transactional revenues to cater to diverse preferences across multiple screens.

items.title: RBI injects ₹1.41 lakh crore liquidity in banking system via 7-day VRR auction
items.summary: The funds were infused at a cut-off and weighted average rate of 5.26%

items.title: Small-cap multibagger stock Blue Cloud Softech Solutions share price jumps 44% in two days. Here’s why
items.summary: Blue Cloud Softech Solutions share price has rallied 36% in one month and gained 38% in three months. The smallcap stock has risen 3% in six months, while it has added 19% on a YTD basis. Blue Cloud Softech Solutions share price has delivered multibagger returns of 465% over the past five years.

items.title: Euro zone yields fall as investors cut bets on ECB hikes
items.summary: European government bond yields dipped Tuesday, finding stability after Monday's surge. Investors are scaling back expectations of further European Central Bank rate hikes following President Lagarde's remarks, while the US Federal Reserve is anticipated to continue its tightening path. This divergence has widened the borrowing cost gap between German and US two-year debt significantly.

items.title: Auto OEM vehicle launches delayed by 9-15 months despite digital investments: Study
items.summary: Indian carmakers are facing significant delays, averaging 9-15 months, in launching new vehicles despite heavy investment in digital tools. A new study reveals most issues stem from the design phase, where early digital resolution could save time and money. Problems often surface late in physical builds, with experts suggesting a shift towards earlier digital validation and collaboration to prevent these costly setbacks.

items.title: National developers increase share in NCR real estate market
items.summary: National real estate developers are significantly increasing their footprint in the NCR region, capturing over 13% of new residential supply by 2025-end, up from just 3% in 2022. This surge reflects a growing preference for institutionalized, brand-driven projects among homebuyers seeking trust and strong execution. Major players like Godrej Properties are leading this expansion, focusing on premium and luxury segments, particularly in Gurugram, as infrastructure development fuels market evolution.

items.title: Alan Greenspan: champion of neoliberal finance
items.summary: For nearly two decades, Alan Greenspan was the most influential central banker on the planet. His death invites a fresh look at the ideas that transformed, and shook the global economy

items.title: Turtlemint IPO Day 3: Subscribed 56% by midday, retail investors lead demand
items.summary: The retail investors’ portion was subscribed 0.78 times, while the qualified institutional buyers category received bids for 0.73 times the shares reserved

items.title: The Supreme Court’s Amazon ruling has revealed a gap in the CCI’s authority over corporate mergers
items.summary: The apex court's recent verdict on the Amazon-Future case has revealed a vulnerability of the Competition Commission of India (CCI) to deception in merger reviews. India should tighten its merger control regime.

items.title: Innov8 acquires Vatika Business Centres to expand co-working biz
items.summary: Coworking firm Innov8 has acquired Vatika Business Centres, significantly expanding its footprint across India. This strategic move integrates 11 prime business centres in major cities, bolstering Innov8's presence in key commercial hubs. The acquisition aims to enhance client value through expanded offerings and improved technology, aligning with Innov8's vision for a leading premium workspace platform.

items.title: Jivial Industries IPO: Issue sees tepid response on first bidding day. Check GMP, key details to know
items.summary: Jivial Industries IPO launched on 23 June and ends on 25 June, priced at  ₹196 per share. The company manufactures aluminium systems and plans to enhance capacity with a new plant. It aims to raise  ₹31.99 crore through the IPO for expansion and corporate purposes.

items.title: Cement makers strive to repair realizations; can investor sentiment revive?
items.summary: Premium cement and higher trade sales are supporting realizations, but rising fuel costs, weak pricing power and looming capacity additions are clouding earnings prospects.

items.title: Should investors buy the dip in Vedanta shares after 9% slide? Analysts weigh in
items.summary: Vedanta shares fell on Tuesday after promoter entity Twin Star Holdings reportedly sold around 7.3 crore shares (1.7% stake) worth ₹2,149 crore through a block deal. Analysts said the stock has turned technically weak after breaking below key support levels and its 100-day EMA, with bearish signals from RSI and MACD indicators.

items.title: Shreedhar Spinners IPO Day 1: Here's GMP, subscription status, and other details
items.summary: The company plans to raise  ₹30.68 crore through a fresh issue of 57.88 lakh equity shares. The IPO price band has been set at  ₹51–53 per share, with the stock slated to debut on the NSE SME platform on July 1.

items.title: Central India may get two-week sowing window before next monsoon lull
items.summary: The current week (June 22-29) and the one that follows (June 29-July 6) may offer the best opportunity for sowing, while prospects during July 13-20 are less encouraging

items.title: Small-cap stock Interarch Building climbs over 3% after announcing new orders worth  ₹375 crore in June; details here
items.summary: Interarch Building Solutions has secured new orders worth  ₹375 crore in June. Among the key projects secured in June are four large-scale industrial projects covering a plinth area of approximately 200,000 sq. m.

items.title: Bharti Airtel raises stake in Airtel Africa to 79 per cent in transaction worth Rs 28,000 cr
items.summary: Bharti Airtel has significantly boosted its stake in Airtel Africa to approximately 79 percent through a Rs 28,200 crore share-swap deal. This strategic move, involving a cashless exchange with promoter group entity ICIL, enhances Bharti Airtel's economic interest in the high-growth African subsidiary. The company anticipates Airtel Africa becoming a USD 10 billion revenue entity, contributing substantial returns.

items.title: Rupee flat as traders mull Fed rate path, keep eyes peeled for flow clues
items.summary: The Indian rupee held steady as falling oil prices provided some relief, though traders remained cautious due to anticipated US Federal Reserve rate hikes. While policy measures have helped the rupee recover, the prospect of higher US interest rates poses a challenge. Analysts are closely watching the impact of these measures on dollar inflows, with some predicting significant amounts.

items.title: India's LPG imports from U.S. set to cross 1 million tons in June, sources say
items.summary: Imports of LPG, widely used as cooking fuel in Indian households, declined to as ‌low as 696,000 tons in April because of the strait blockade, government data ‌showeD

items.title: No bids made in Indian Oil Corp tender for ships to carry Gulf cargoes
items.summary: Shipowners stay cautious amid uncertainty over transit through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Goyal, US Trade Representative Greer begin talks on trade pact
items.summary: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal and India's Chief Negotiator Darpan Jain, who is also Additional Secretary in the Department of Commerce, are attending the meeting

items.title: Advit Jewels IPO sails through on Day 1, subscribed over 6.5 times. GMP signals 47% listing upside
items.summary: Jaipur's Advit Jewels IPO is off to a flying start, oversubscribed 2.3 times on Day 1, with strong retail investor interest. The grey market signals a potential 47% listing gain. The Rs 165 crore issue will fund working capital and debt repayment.  The handcrafted jewellery maker, operating under the Rambhajo brand, aims for a July 1 stock market debut.

items.title: Anil Singhvi Market Strategy: Nifty50 profit-booking area at 24,300-24,400, Nifty Bank at 58,475-58,725
items.summary: Anil Singhvi Nifty50 and Nifty Bank Strategy: For existing long positions in the Nifty 50, Zee Business Managing Editor Anil Singhvi recommends placing an intraday stop loss at 23,900 and a closing stop loss at 24,000.

items.title: Biosimilars and innovation will drive Indian pharma's next growth phase: Vishal Manchanda
items.summary: India's pharmaceutical sector is pivoting beyond generics, with companies aggressively pursuing growth in biosimilars, nutraceuticals, and consumer healthcare. While US FDA approvals for complex generics remain a challenge, innovation and contract manufacturing offer significant future profit pools. Experts anticipate biosimilars could generate substantial revenue, and despite a slow start, GLP-1 drugs show long-term promise. Sun Pharma is highlighted as a preferred domestic investment.

items.title: An industrial risk the Qatar blast shares with accidents in India | Explained
items.summary: Industry data consistently show that nearly 50% of all process safety incidents occur during 10% of an industrial plant’s operations, when it is in a transient mode

items.title: Waterways IPO: 10 positives Anil Singhvi wants bidders to know about Cordelia cruise operator
items.summary: Waterways Leisure IPO: The initial public offering (IPO) of Waterways Leisure Tourism Limited opened for public bidding on Tuesday, June 23 and will remain open till June 25. The issue is entirely a fresh issue of 0.72 crore shares.

items.title: India bonds rangebound as oil-led rally loses steam
items.summary: Indian government bonds held steady as a recent rally paused, with investors now awaiting cues from Bloomberg's index inclusion decision and the monsoon's impact.  Yields on the benchmark 2036 note saw a slight dip.  Expectations of foreign inflows, boosted by potential index inclusion and easing oil prices, are supporting the market, though El Nino's effect on inflation and growth remains a watchpoint.

items.title: PFC becomes first non-bank lender to tap dollar debt after RBI subsidy, bankers say
items.summary: PFC accepts bid worth $300 ​million for a five-year dollar bond issue

items.title: Bitcoin trades near $64,000, extends June consolidation amid cautious market sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin remained under pressure and traded near the $64,000 level, extending June’s consolidation as cautious sentiment and ETF outflows weighed on markets. However, improving on-chain activity, falling exchange balances and key technical support levels suggest investors continue positioning for the next directional move.

items.title: Game theory analysis of delimitation: political power doesn’t stem only from a state’s share of seats in Parliament
items.summary: India’s debate on delimitation has focused on its reallotment of seats in Parliament and each state’s share of these. But the exercise would shift the relative political power of states in a far more complex way. Here's a game theory analysis.

items.title: Syrma SGS Technology shares jump 5% after JV pact with Japan’s Kaga Electronics
items.summary: Syrma SGS Technology's shares jumped nearly 5% after announcing a significant joint venture with Japan's Kaga Electronics. This collaboration aims to establish a cutting-edge manufacturing facility in India, primarily serving Japanese clients and bolstering Syrma's presence in high-value electronics. The partnership, with Syrma holding a 60% stake, is seen as a strategic move to capitalize on global supply chain diversification and India's growing appeal for Japanese manufacturing expansion.

items.title: CSM Technologies IPO opens on Wednesday: GMP, other key details of  ₹146 crore public issue in 10 points
items.summary: CSM Technologies is set to open its  ₹146 crore IPO on June 24, aiming to raise funds for working capital, debt payments, and expansion. CSM Technologies IPO will list on the BSE and the NSE on Thursday, 2 July.

items.title: What income makes you middle class in India? Here’s what data from NSE DRHP shows
items.summary: In FY26, households earning above  ₹8 lakh accounted for 34.38% of all households. This share is projected to rise to 42.74% by FY31. This means that nearly four out of every ten Indian households could be earning more than  ₹8 lakh annually within the next five years.

items.title: AI crash coming closer? South Korea’s Kospi plunges 10%, triggers trading halt
items.summary: South Korean stocks experienced a sharp decline, with the Kospi index plummeting nearly 10% from its record high. Chip giants SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics led the sell-off as investors grew concerned about an overstretched rally. This downturn follows a period of strong gains, with market watchers now closely awaiting Micron Technology's earnings for insights into the semiconductor sector's health. Foreign investors offloaded significant holdings amid rising market volatility.

items.title: No bids made in Indian Oil tender for ships to carry Gulf cargoes, say sources
items.summary: Indian Oil Corporation has failed to secure any bids for chartering vessels to transport crude oil and LPG from the Strait of Hormuz.  Ship owners are hesitant due to the current geopolitical climate, preferring to await clearer terms before entering the region.  The tenders sought to move significant quantities of LPG and crude from key Middle Eastern ports to India's west coast.

items.title: In the pan-India movie era, cultural missteps can cost stars more than court cases
items.summary: In the pan-India movie era, a celebrity’s biggest risk may not be a court case but cultural backlash. Social media can turn local grievances into national controversies, damaging endorsements, trust and regional audience goodwill.

items.title: Gujarat regulator holds right-of-way obstruction can qualify as force majeure
items.summary: GERC rules right-of-way issues can qualify as force majeure, allowing project delays without penalties for developers

items.title: IDFC First scam: CBI arrests senior IAS officer for embezzling ₹60 crore
items.summary: The case is part of a CBI investigation into embezzlement of ₹657 crore from the accounts of the Haryana government and Chandigarh administration maintained at IDFC First Bank

items.title: From record IPO to sharp correction: Why SpaceX shares are sliding
items.summary: SpaceX’s post-rally decline shifted investor focus from momentum to execution as concerns grew over debt issuance, elevated AI spending and valuation. While long-term optimism around AI, Starlink and space ambitions remains intact, markets are increasingly demanding clearer paths to profitability and disciplined capital allocation.

items.title: Vedanta Power, Oil & Gas, and Iron shares rally up to 5%; Aluminium sheds 3%. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: Vedanta’s newly demerged entities extended their rally, with Oil & Gas, Power and Iron & Steel hitting upper circuits, while Aluminium slipped despite Citi’s bullish outlook. Brokerages remain constructive on select businesses, though views differ on valuations as investors assess growth, cyclicality and execution risks.

items.title: Jefferies initiates coverage on GE Vernova, bullish on 2 other power transmission stocks
items.summary: Jefferies has initiated coverage on GE Vernova T&D India with a Hold rating and a target price of ₹6,000, citing limited upside despite strong sector tailwinds. The brokerage remains bullish on Hitachi Energy India and Siemens Energy India, reiterating Buy ratings with target prices of ₹43,145 and ₹4,500, respectively.

items.title: US bond yields back to 'normal'; AI needs a reality check, says Ed Yardeni
items.summary: Veteran strategist Ed Yardeni views the recent rise in Treasury yields and the AI stock correction as healthy market adjustments, not crisis signals. He believes bond yields are returning to normal levels and AI stock valuations are undergoing a realistic reassessment. Despite a hawkish Fed stance, Yardeni anticipates only one or two rate hikes in the next year, though this could pressure emerging economies.

items.title: Healthium Medtech CEO Anish Bafna steps down, moves to non-executive chairman role
items.summary: Healthium Medtech's CEO Anish Bafna is transitioning to non-executive chairman as KKR-owned company seeks a new chief within a month. Bafna, instrumental in Healthium's growth and acquisitions since 2018, will aid in successor selection. Despite record revenue, the medical devices firm saw profits decline recently, facing market competition and a slowdown in the Indian surgical products sector.

items.title: Will AI talent departures hurt Alphabet's long-term growth?
items.summary: Alphabet shares fell sharply after Google DeepMind lost top AI researchers, including John Jumper to Anthropic, sparking investor concerns about talent retention and competitive edge versus OpenAI and rivals market.

items.title: Micron, Anthropic sign AI infrastructure supply agreement
items.summary: Financial terms of the supply agreement ​and Micron’s Series H investment in Anthropic were not disclosed

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech IPO: Last day to bid! Should you apply?
items.summary: Turtlemint Fintech IPO: The initial public offering (IPO) of Turtlemint Fintech Solutions Ltd has stepped into the third and last day of public bidding today, June 23, with the issue subscribed 54 per cent, as of 11 am, according to the provisional exchange data. Earlier, market expert Anil Singhvi, Managing Editor at Zee Business, shared his insights on whether to apply for the IPO or not.

items.title: Tax reforms to play vital role in attracting global capital to India: Experts
items.summary: India's economic ascent hinges on predictable and simplified tax reforms to attract global capital, according to a senior financial expert. The proposed Income Tax Code aims for a modern, user-friendly framework, aligning with international standards. The ICAI Singapore Chapter highlighted strides in transparent, tech-driven tax administration, boosting India's appeal as an investment hub through regulatory improvements and a stable policy environment.

items.title: ET Graphics: The crown in 2025 goes to a royal whisky brand
items.summary: In a significant shift, Royal Stag has claimed the title of the world's largest-selling whisky brand by volume, dethroning McDowell's after a decade. This milestone highlights the surging growth of Indian-made foreign liquor, which now constitutes over 25% of global large spirits brands. Six of the top ten whisky brands are now Indian, underscoring the nation's growing influence in the international spirits market.

Malhotra highlighted the need for financial institutions to leverage digital infrastructure like Account Aggregator and ULI for efficient, data-driven credit.

items.title: RBI MPC: Growth rate could top 7% if oil prices fall further

The Reserve Bank of India has projected FY27 growth at 6.6% in the June policy, while the economy expanded at 7.7% in the January-March quarter, exceeding earlier estimates.

items.title: Tata Electronics faces cybersecurity breach; says no impact on business

The company stated that its operations remain unaffected and response protocols were immediately activated. The leaked data reportedly includes over 200,000 files, encompassing emails and employee passport copies.

items.title: Drugmakers yet to see gains from government's minimum import price policy

Despite setting price thresholds for key raw materials like penicillin G, industry executives report a lack of clear beneficial impact. Buyers and manufacturers are hesitant to shift to domestic suppliers at MIP-linked prices, leaving significant production capacity underutilized. Some anticipate this is a temporary phase as existing stockpiles are consumed.

However, robust growth in electricity generation, driven by high temperatures and renewable energy, prevented a steeper fall. Cement and steel sectors showed steady expansion, supported by infrastructure development.

items.summary: "Allowing imports of cement from Pakistan, therefore, carried with it the additional risk in that it provides an effective cover for smuggling of contraband goods and harmful weapons and ammunition concealed in cement bags which comes in rakes and trucks, in the hands of disruptionist elements," he said.

items.title: Moneycontrol Exclusive: Govt plans overhaul of tax administration for smooth GST rollout
items.summary: The government has readied plans for a major change in India’s tax administration structure, including overhauling the Central Board of Excise and Customs (CBEC) to ensure a glitch-free roll-out of a nation-wide goods and services tax (GST).

items.title: Sensex, Nifty wobbly; Hind Zinc, SBI, Force Motors most active
items.summary: Asian Paints, SBI, Tata Motors, HUL and Maruti Suzuki are top gainers while ONGC, Axis Bank, MM, Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp are major losers in the Sensex.

items.title: Uber  redBus partner with ixigo for train travellers
items.summary: ixigo, India’s leading mobile travel marketplace has announced partnerships with Uber and redBus to enable instant bookings for cabs and buses natively within ixigo trains app.

items.title: Workload in IT to come down due to AI; jobs to reduce: Genpact
items.summary: But along with that the employment growth rates have started to dip, NV 'Tiger' Tyagarajan says. The percentage addition of employees in India will not be same as past. And due to advancements, a workforce with higher skill sets is required for the IT industry, he says.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.

items.title: Up over 200% in 1 year, what's buzzing with Borosil Glass?

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
